title: Integer overflow possible in encode_serial_frame return value
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/meshcore/codec.c
Line: 70-71

```c
return (int)(payload_len + 3U);
```

If CONFIG_LICHEN_MESHCORE_MAX_SERIAL_PAYLOAD is larger than INT_MAX - 3, the addition could exceed INT_MAX. Casting a size_t larger than INT_MAX to int is implementation-defined behavior.

While unlikely in practice (embedded systems rarely have such large payloads), there is no compile-time check to prevent misconfiguration.

Fix: Add BUILD_ASSERT(CONFIG_LICHEN_MESHCORE_MAX_SERIAL_PAYLOAD <= INT_MAX - 3, "Serial payload too large for int return");
notes: Commit: f3b1f704f (assert already present per description)
close_reason: BUILD_ASSERT already implemented in codec.c:18-19 matching the described fix. Verified Kconfig range and asserts prevent overflow.
